calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Bovarnick, Esq., for Jeffrey Stephenson, Steven Neves, Oliver Lowe, Patrick Newhart (individually), and Jonah Roll (individually) via zoom..
James Monroe Allen, Esq., for and with Jonah Roll, Petitioner via zoom.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated July 7, 2022 is admitted to probate. Jonah Roll is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 19, 2027 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Michael Kasolas is appointed Probate Referee. Disclosure: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cece. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Continued To Apr-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Isabella Shrammel, Esq., for Jeffrey Stephenson, Steven Neves, Oliver Lowe, Patrick Newhart (individually), and Jonah Roll (individually) via zoom.
James Monroe Allen, Esq., for and with Jonah Roll, Petitioner via zoom.

The Court shall sign the Order extending Special Administration and Authorizing Clerk to Issue Letter of Extended Special Administration. The special administration of the Estate of George David by Jonah Roll as special administrator is hereby extended "until further order of the Court".

The matter is continued to April 1, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Deleon. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Oct-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following related matters are heard concurrently: PTR-23-306616 and PES-24-307907.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Lisa Tan, Esq., for and with Carl Feinberg, Respondent.
Isabella Shrammel, Esq., for Jeffrey Stephenson, Steven Neves, Oliver Lowe, Patrick Newhart (individually), and Jonah Roll (individually).

James Monroe Allen, Esq., in court, for and with Jonah Roll, Petitioner.

At the court's query, Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the status of the related petitions. Mr. Allen stated the needs of extending the special administration, that there's $250,000.00 in dividend checks but only $37,000.00 was deposited, and that the remainder were stale checks.

The Court shall issue a Breslin Order for the trust and estate matters. The matters in both cases are continued to October 29, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Ng. Not reported.
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2025 Continued To May-09-25 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following related matters are heard concurrently: PES-21-304412 and PTR-24-307706.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Lisa Tan, Esq., for and with Carl Feinberg, Respondent.

Sarah Brooks, Esq., for Jeffrey Stephenson, Steven Neves, Oliver Lowe, Patrick Newhart (individually), and Jonah Roll (individually).

James Monroe Allen, Esq., in court, and Steve Bovarnick, Esq., via Zoom video conference, for and with Jonah Roll, Petitioner.

At the court's query, Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the status of the related petitions, status of discovery, the failure to personally serve notice to Robert Beles and Joseph James Phillipp, III. The court confirms with Ms. Tan that her client does contest the entire will and offers advocacy for her client's position. There is discussion of whether the parties have met and conferred regarding resolving the matters. Counsel advises that additional Discovery is required to have meaningful settlement discussions. Mr. Allen reports as to his reason for filing the 850 petition. Mr. Bovarnick reports that he does not object to the 850 petition. Ms. Tan advises that she has concerns with the lack of service of the 850 petition and requests that the court consider not granting it accordingly. The pending petition for special administrator is discussed. Mr. Bovarnick advises of the intent to file a new Petition for Appointment of Special Administrator.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Jul-23-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The court advises that if no settlement discussions are held before the next calling, it will consider issuing a Breslin order.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Mar-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-16-2024 Continued To Jan-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Allen for and with Jonah Roll, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jeffrey R. Loew for Carl Feinberg, Objector (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding recently filed objections submitted by Carl Feinberg. The court determined that the instant petition could not be granted at this time and proposed a continuance to allow the petitioner to review and respond to the objection. The court and counsel agree to continue the matter to January 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to align with related trust matter scheduling for hearing on that date (PTR-23-306616).

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
